item.title: Sable Offshore heads to federal court in bid to defeat California
item.description: The company is in the middle of a battle pitting the Trump administration against California energy policy

item.title: Majors steer clear of Alaska wildlife refuge lease sale
item.description: Five of 58 tracts were sold, mostly to a state-owned development corporation

item.title: Operator confirms gas ‘presence’ in promising Andaman offshore exploration well
item.description: Indian operator has now established presence of hydrocarbons at two of three exploration wells it has drilled in Andaman basin

item.title: Barbados launches bid round for 19 ultra-deepwater blocks
item.description: Caribbean nation hopes to attract international players seeking new offshore acreage

item.title: Iraqi Kurdistan-focused player completes listing switch to Oslo
item.description: Company completes previously-announced plan to transfer primary listing from Toronto

item.title: Eni signs up to explore ex-BP licence in Gambia
item.description: Italian major makes its debut in small African nation

item.title: Spend Strait of Hormuz windfall profits on opportunities too long ignored
item.description: As beneficiaries of the crisis, oil and gas companies should consider investing in a far different energy future

item.title: ConocoPhillips set to plunge into West African frontier
item.description: US player eyes Equatorial Guinea blocks near Sao Tome acreage that hosted Galp Energia's Jaca-1 wildcat

item.title: Japanese player to take stake in Mexico-bound floater
item.description: SBM Offshore divesting interest in Chalchi FSO to Nippon Yusen Kabushiki Kaisha

item.title: Equinor shows clear intent to accelerate seven recent discoveries
item.description: Norwegian giant has new philosophy around faster development of near-field discoveries

item.title: BP steps up Caspian exploration push
item.description: Top executive sheds light on supermajor’s next steps for keenly watched Caspian prospects

item.title: Velesto lands jack-up work in Thailand
item.description: Northern Gulf Petroleum has firm seven-well campaign

item.title: Australian FSRU clears environmental approvals
item.description: Viva Energy progresses with FSRU plan after clearing state and federal environmental approval

item.title: This week's leading oil and gas appointments
FPSO rivals locked in close contest for major Indonesia project
item.description: Bumi Armada and Yinson Production competing for Tangkulo contract

item.title: Final bids due for new UK FPSO
item.description: Next week's final price offers mark real progress for Cambo. But even after a bidder is chosen — likely within three months— the project still lacks the final investment decision and environmental approval

item.title: Adnoc closing in on multibillion-dollar awards for major offshore gas project
item.description: Leaders have emerged for the three Umm Shaif gas cap packages, and operator Adnoc is likely to place awards within weeks

item.title: French contractor appoints Subsea7 veteran as new chief executive
item.description: Olivier Blaringhem set to lead Bourbon starting in September

item.title: Global LNG tightness could trigger unprecedented demand destruction
item.description: Asia now outbidding Europe for LNG cargoes in name of security of supply but painful demand destruction rebalancing set to hit both markets

item.title: ‘Supply chains are under strain’: How Trafigura’s agile tanker fleet turned chaos into profit
item.description: Swiss trader says it has the capacity to react swiftly to market disruption

item.title: Galp makes exploration move in Equatorial Guinea
item.description: Portuguese player signs tentative deal to study three blocks

item.title: Eni snaps up huge reconnaissance licence in undrilled African frontier
item.description: Italian major secures rights to study 50,000 square kilometre tract in Guinea-Conakry

item.title: Alberta lands oil export deal with Ottawa, then flirts with separatism
item.description: Icy relations between province and federal government may be thawing, but independence movement could spoil the mood

item.title: Russia allows TotalEnergies to exit sanctioned LNG project
item.description: French supermajor given green light to offload its 10% stake in Arctic LNG 2 to Russian company

item.title: Industry gears up for contentious Alaska lease sale
item.description: US Bureau of Land Management pledges to open more of Alaska and beyond to drilling amid record leasing activity

item.title: Transocean semisub snapped up for exploration Down Under
item.description: Santos and Carnarvon planning 2027 Bedout sub-basin campaign, with drilling targets including untested plays

item.title: Asia-focused indie hunting for a new CEO
item.description: Miltos Xynogalas stepping back from the helm of Conrad Asia Energy

item.title: Asia-focused indie hunting for new CEO
item.description: Miltos Xynogalas stepping back from helm of Conrad Asia Energy

item.title: Contractors eye options to help unlock future LNG projects
item.description: Contractors discuss future of LNG contract and execution models during Samsung E&A Tech Forum 2026

item.title: Cambodia moves offshore dispute with Thailand to the UN
item.description: Phnom Penh government keen to unlock oil and gas resources in Overlapping Claims Area
